response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with strong web, mobile, and low-latency systems credentials, but his profile has almost no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His low-latency work at Sociomantic is the closest data point, but it concerns HTTP-layer software performance, not hardware or silicon-level analysis. The seniority and education thresholds are nominally met (Bachelor's + 17 years), but the domain mismatch is fundamental and not bridgeable through general engineering experience alone.
strengths: 17+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and low-latency domains, demonstrating adaptability and technical depth.
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering: async I/O, fiber-based concurrency, and caching in high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic).
Solid systems-level exposure including C/C++ (NI, wave field synthesis thesis), embedded tooling for robotic arms, and cross-platform infrastructure work.
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware-software co-design — the core technical pillars of this role.
No background in AI/ML, deep neural network training or inference, GPU architecture, or any accelerator-related workloads.
No SIMD/MIMD programming, compiler-level optimization, or silicon performance analysis visible anywhere in the resume or project history.
